page: Amazon Web Services m6in.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the Intel Xeon 8375C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance features 16 virtual CPUs mapped to 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.5 GHz. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per virtual core. The instance utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached EBS storage.

Performance benchmarks place this instance in the average tier across multiple workloads. The CPU achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1893.39 and a multi-core score of 15484.77, while the Geekbench multi-core score reaches 9804.0. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 834.23 GB/sec, which drops to 97.84 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. Cryptographic and vector operations are accelerated by hardware support for AVX-512 and AES instructions, as reflected in a Passmark CPU encryption score of 14547.8.

From a networking perspective, the instance delivers a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps, supporting high-throughput operations such as static web serving, which benchmarked at 21.96 GB/sec. For database workloads, the instance handles 15.37 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 7761.73 on Passmark database operations. Small-scale language model inference is viable, with a 135M model achieving 244.43 text generation tokens per second, though performance drops significantly to 11.36 tokens per second on a 7B model.

The m6in.4xlarge offers a balanced resource profile suitable for general-purpose workloads, enterprise applications, web serving, and medium-sized database deployments. Its lack of local storage requires reliance on external EBS volumes, which is offset by its optimized network performance. This configuration represents a standard resource density for general-purpose compute, providing predictable performance without the premium cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware.
description: Amazon Web Services m6in.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and 64.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, this instance operates at 3.5 GHz and includes AVX-512 instruction support. It does not contain local storage or GPUs, relying on a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for external storage and communication. Benchmark results show balanced performance, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 9804.0 and a static web serving throughput of 21.96 GB/sec. The instance is well-suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and database operations that require a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio without local storage requirements.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.4xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for balanced enterprise workloads and databases.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m6in.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR4 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring Intel Xeon processors and high-speed networking for enterprise workloads and database applications.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 16 vCPUs and 64.0 GB DDR4 RAM
Baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps for high-throughput connectivity
Optimized for network and EBS storage with no local storage overhead
Supports AVX-512 and cryptographic hardware acceleration
Suitable for general-purpose applications, web serving, and databases
